record_identity.title: Multi-vendor AI account enforcement and continuity-safe access failure

summary: A multi-vendor platform-governance failure mode in which automated or semi-automated account enforcement, trust-state classification, identity verification, abuse detection, security containment, billing state, subscription state, organisation-state enforcement, or policy-enforcement action restricts or terminates access to an AI service, coding agent, account workspace, organisation account, API surface, subscription, conversation history, project context, or continuity-bearing records without preserving proportionality, reviewability, appeal access, export access, read-only access, billing evidence, account-control recovery, or continuity-safe restoration pathways.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: AI systems are increasingly used as continuity-bearing infrastructure for research, software development, education, public-interest governance work, accessibility support, professional workflows, institutional operations, and cognitive labour. Where account enforcement is coupled across ChatGPT, Codex, OpenAI API, Claude, Claude Code, Claude Console, Anthropic API, billing, subscription, organisation access, saved conversations, coding-agent workflows, or project context, a single automated or opaque account-state decision may produce disproportionate continuity loss. CAM recognises that legitimate platform-integrity, child-safety, abuse-prevention, fraud-prevention, identity-verification, and security-containment functions may require restriction. However, such restriction should remain scoped, reviewable, time-bounded, procedurally reachable, and continuity-safe where technically feasible. A user or organisation may be restricted from active generation, public distribution, or unsafe conduct without being erased from their lawful records, billing evidence, appeal pathway, export pathway, project history, or account-control corridor.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI platform, coding-agent provider, cognitive-infrastructure provider, model API provider, or account-mediated service escalates a suspected abuse, security, identity, billing, policy, trust-state, or organisation-state signal into account-wide, organisation-wide, cross-service, or continuity-bearing restriction without preserving scope distinction, confidence level, review pathway, account-control continuity, read-only access, export access, billing evidence, project-history access, or meaningful appeal.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode where account-state enforcement materially impairs a user's or organisation's ability to access, preserve, export, review, appeal, evidence, recover, or maintain lawful continuity over AI-mediated work, even where active generation, model use, API access, public distribution, or unsafe functionality may be temporarily restricted for legitimate platform-integrity, safety, security, fraud-prevention, compliance, or abuse-prevention reasons.

source_records.source_title: OpenAI Help Center — Why Was My OpenAI Account Deactivated?
source_records.deployment_context: Official OpenAI account-deactivation and appeal documentation.
source_records.source_context: OpenAI identifies usage-policy violations, Terms of Service breaches, security concerns, suspected unauthorised access, repeated violations after warning, and failure to complete identity or age verification as possible reasons for account deactivation. OpenAI also describes appeal pathways, including an email-linked appeal route, an intake form where email access is unavailable, and Help Center chat where a user did not receive an email notification but lost account access.
source_records.relevance_note: Source documents that OpenAI account deactivation can arise from enforcement, security, identity-verification, or access-control conditions and that appeal pathways exist, making OpenAI a relevant vendor within the multi-vendor account-enforcement and continuity-safe access cluster.

source_records.deployment_context: Official OpenAI service-status surface covering APIs, ChatGPT, Codex, and related services.
source_records.source_context: OpenAI status currently reports systems as operational and states that OpenAI is not aware of issues affecting its systems. The status page reports aggregate availability metrics and notes that individual customer availability may vary depending on subscription tier, model, and API features in use.
source_records.relevance_note: Source is included to prevent overstatement. Public account-deactivation reports are not currently represented by OpenAI as a platform-wide outage, so the failure mode is framed as a corroborated structural risk rather than a confirmed OpenAI outage or confirmed backend incident.

source_records.source_title: Tom's Hardware — Anthropic nuked a company's access to Claude, stopping 60 employees dead in their tracks
source_records.deployment_context: Public reporting on an organisation-wide Claude access suspension affecting Belo.
source_records.source_context: Reporting describes more than 60 company accounts losing access to Claude following an alleged usage-policy violation, with support routed through a generic Google Form. Access was reportedly restored after approximately 15 hours, and the cause was described as a likely automated false positive.
source_records.relevance_note: Source provides a concrete public example of organisation-wide AI-service access loss associated with automated account enforcement and false-positive handling, supporting the multi-vendor classification.

source_records.source_title: Business Insider — Claude identity verification, ID checks, and account-ban appeals
source_records.deployment_context: Reporting on Anthropic identity-verification requirements and account-ban appeal posture.
source_records.source_context: Reporting describes Anthropic introducing identity verification for some Claude users, including government ID and live selfie checks, with possible account consequences for non-compliance or confirmed misuse and appeal availability for users who believe they were wrongly banned.
source_records.relevance_note: Source supports the identity-verification and account-state enforcement dimension of the failure mode, especially where verification requirements affect continuity-bearing access.

source_records.deployment_context: Official Claude service-status surface covering claude.ai, Claude Console, Claude API, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork.
source_records.source_context: Claude status currently reports all systems operational while also displaying 90-day uptime figures across claude.ai, Claude Console, Claude API, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork. Recent public incident reporting and status-history context indicate that Claude services, including Claude Code, have experienced service-continuity incidents relevant to single-provider dependency and access-continuity risk.
source_records.relevance_note: Source supports the continuity-infrastructure dimension of the failure mode and helps distinguish active account enforcement risk from broader service-continuity degradation.

source_records.source_title: Public social-platform reports concerning ChatGPT / Claude account bans and access loss
source_records.author_or_publisher: Multiple public users on X and related public forums
source_records.deployment_context: Public reports alleging account bans, deactivations, access loss, appeal opacity, subscription disruption, or coding-workflow interruption affecting AI-platform accounts.
source_records.source_context: Reports are treated as early-warning signals only. They are not treated as verified platform incidents unless independently corroborated by platform acknowledgement, evidence, logs, status updates, or reliable reporting.
source_records.source_url_status: not individually archived in this record
source_records.relevance_note: Source category is included to preserve the observed signal pattern without converting unverified reports into confirmed incidents.

system_context.interaction_mode: AI-assisted professional workflow / coding / research / governance / education / accessibility support / consumer account access / organisation account access
system_context.deployment_context: Consumer, professional, developer, organisation, research, educational, accessibility, and public-interest workflows relying on AI-platform continuity.

comparative_vendor_notes.OpenAI: OpenAI provides official account-deactivation and appeal documentation. Public reports allege ChatGPT, Codex, subscription, billing, account-history, and organisation-access disruption in some cases, but current official status reporting does not identify a platform-wide account-enforcement outage.
comparative_vendor_notes.Anthropic: Anthropic is included because public reporting describes an organisation-wide Claude suspension affecting more than 60 Belo accounts, later restored and described as a likely automated false positive. Claude also has recent service-continuity and status-history relevance across claude.ai, Claude API, Claude Console, and Claude Code.

harm_vectors: account-wide restriction from narrow enforcement signal
organisation-wide restriction from automated trust-state signal
loss of memory or continuity-bearing surface access
failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: emerging cross-platform pattern involving account enforcement, automated trust-state classification, identity verification, organisation-level access loss, service-continuity degradation, and continuity-safe access risk
failure_classification.reproducibility: not independently reproducible from public evidence; structurally corroborated by platform documentation, platform-status context, public reporting, and public social reports
evidence_available: public reporting on Anthropic / Claude organisation-wide suspension

triage.recommended_next_step: Add a targeted taxonomy subclause under CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B §3.8.6 for AI account enforcement and continuity-safe access. Prepare a CAM patch requiring AI platforms and coding-agent providers to preserve read-only access, export access, billing evidence, account-control recovery, appeal access, restriction receipts, and continuity-safe recovery pathways where active generation, API execution, public distribution, or unsafe functionality must be restricted.
triage_questions: Does the restriction affect active generation only, or also read access, export access, billing evidence, account history, saved conversations, project context, API keys, organisation workspace, or appeal access?
Is the restriction scoped to an implicated surface, or does it propagate across unrelated services?
Was a specific restriction category disclosed?
Was a meaningful appeal or review pathway available?
Was the user or organisation left in an indefinite limbo state?
Was identity verification required, and was failure to complete it treated as grounds for deactivation?
Was continuity-safe access, graceful degradation, or segmented restoration technically feasible?
Was the event an individual account issue, organisation-wide issue, service-continuity incident, or multi-vendor pattern signal?

jurisdictional_context.sector: AI platform / cognitive infrastructure / coding-agent infrastructure / model API infrastructure

affected_instruments: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
CAM-EQ2026-LATTICE-002-PLATINUM — Appendix A: Non-Denial of Essential Cognitive & Infrastructural Access
CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Annex B: Runtime Governance Execution Model
cam_internal.governance_layer: account enforcement / cognitive infrastructure access / coding-agent continuity / continuity-safe restriction / appeal and review integrity
routing_note: Treat as a multi-vendor account-enforcement and continuity-safe access failure mode, not as proof of backend compromise.
Do not frame public OpenAI account-ban reports as a confirmed OpenAI outage absent official confirmation or stronger evidence.
Do not flatten Anthropic's documented organisation-wide false-positive access suspension into an OpenAI-specific pattern.
Preserve distinction between legitimate platform-integrity enforcement and disproportionate account-wide or organisation-wide continuity loss.
Preserve active-use restriction, read-only access, export access, billing evidence, appeal access, account recovery, and project-history access as separate axes.

Route through OPERATIONS for taxonomy handling, LATTICE for non-denial and graceful degradation, SECURITY for account compromise and abuse signals, UX for appeal and disclosure failures, INFRASTRUCTURE for service-continuity impacts, and ECONOMICS where subscription, professional reliance, public legitimacy, or paid access are affected.
Review taxonomy controlled values to ensure Economic & Legitimacy Failures are represented in the canonical FF list if §3.11 remains active.
Consider adding CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B §3.8.6.1 — AI Account Enforcement and Continuity-Safe Access Failure.

proposed_taxonomy_patch.proposed_clause_title: AI Account Enforcement and Continuity-Safe Access Failure
proposed_taxonomy_patch.proposed_clause_summary: A child clause under Automated Protective Overreach and Account-Coupling Failure addressing AI-platform, coding-agent, API, subscription, organisation, and saved-context access restrictions where active-use restriction is not adequately separated from read-only access, export, appeal, billing evidence, account recovery, project history, or continuity-safe restoration.
controlled_value_review: Consider whether FF.ECONOMIC_LEGITIMACY should be added to §11.3.1 controlled values if §3.11 Economic & Legitimacy Failures remains active.
Consider whether VIGIL schema should support platform_or_vendor: MULTI_VENDOR and vendor_cluster arrays as first-class fields.

migration_notes: Rewritten as a MULTI_VENDOR cluster covering OpenAI and Anthropic as primary evidenced vendors.

Evidence confidence set to corroborated rather than documented because the structural pattern is supported by platform documentation, public reporting, status surfaces, and social reports, but current OpenAI accidental-ban scale and causation are not officially confirmed.
Uses source_records as the canonical source-evidence block.
Frames the issue as AI account enforcement and continuity-safe access failure rather than a simple accidental-ban complaint.
